title: Calycera viridiflora
taxonomy: Calycera viridiflora is classified within the family Calyceraceae and is a member of the order Asterales. This family is a small group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the southern parts of South America. The order Asterales includes a diverse range of flowering plants, with Calycera viridiflora being one of the few species within the Calyceraceae family. At the kingdom level, Calycera viridiflora is classified as a plant, specifically within the kingdom Plantae. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Calycera viridiflora, also known as the green-flowered calycera, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Calycera viridiflora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calycera viridiflora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of extracts or tinctures to leverage its health benefits.
